title: Backing Up Your Data
drDigitalIntro:
A backup is a copy of your files kept somewhere safe.
• If your device breaks or is stolen, a backup restores everything.
• Back up automatically to an external drive or the cloud.
• The rule is 3-2-1: 3 copies, 2 types of storage, 1 offsite.

playgroundTask.question: Your computer is stolen. You have no backup. What happens to all your photos and documents?

options.text: The company can always recover them for you
They are gone permanently — there's no way to get them back
They are automatically saved in your email
The police can extract them from the stolen laptop

drDigitalSuccess: Without a backup, lost data is usually gone forever. Time Machine or the cloud can save you from this — back up regularly!
drDigitalHint: If the only copy of your data was on the stolen device, and no one else has a copy...
